sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच
एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन् महायोगेश्वरो हरिः।
दर्शयामास पार्थाय परं रूपमैश्वरम्॥
transliteration:
sañjaya uvāca
evaṃ-uktvā tato rājan mahā-yogeśvaro hariḥ
darśayām āsa pārthāya paraṃ rūpam aiśvaram
literal_meaning: Sanjaya said: Having spoken thus, O King, the great Lord of yoga, Hari, revealed to Arjuna His supreme, sovereign form.
contextual_meaning: The moment of disclosure: after preparing Arjuna with words, Krishna now grants direct perceptual access to the archetype of totality, confronting Arjuna with the full spectrum of divine magnificence and terror.
psychological_state: Pre-traumatic growth: the psyche stands at the threshold of an overwhelming expansion that will momentarily shatter habitual self-constructs before reorganizing them at a higher integration.
trigger_scenario: The instant before a life-changing diagnosis, psychedelic journey, or near-death experience when the mind intuits that its current map of reality is about to dissolve.
anchor_text: Behold the whole: beauty and terror together.
